paper_metadata.subject_name: Basics of Electrical and Electronics Engineering

subparts.text: Draw the circuit diagram of practical voltage and practical current source.
Two impedances 𝑍1 = 3 + 𝑗9 Ohm and 𝑍2 = 5 −𝑗9 Ohm are connected in parallel. What is the equivalent impedance of the combination?
Draw the v-i characteristic of p-n junction diode and define peak inverse voltage and breakdown voltage.
Explain the construction and working of junction field effect transistor

questions.question_text: Find branch currents using Nodal analysis for the circuit shown in Figure?

Define Maximum Power Transfer Theorem. What value of resistance may be connected across the terminal a and b, so that Maximum Power transfer takes place through it? Also find the value of Maximum Power transferred.

Two impedances Z1 and Z2 are connected in parallel, where currents corresponding to impedances are 𝐼1 = 50∠10 and 𝐼2 = 20∠300 . If the applied voltage is 100∠15 𝑉, find true power, reactive power and apparent power in each branch.

subparts.text: A series RLC circuit of R=40 Ω, L= 50.07mH and a capacitor is connected across a 400V,50Hz, A.C supply. This RLC combination draws a current of 10A.Calculate Power factor of the circuit. Capacitor value.
An alternating current is expressed as i = 14.14 sin 314t. Determine Maximum current RMS current Frequency Instantaneous current when t = 0.02msec.
Draw and explain the V-I characteristics of a practical PN junction diode
Discuss the applications of a PN Junction Diode.
Explain how a Zener diode may be used as a voltage regulator?
Explain clipping and clamping circuits. Do write some of its examples.
Draw the common emitter circuit and sketch the input and output characteristics. Also explain active region, cutoff region and saturation region by indicating them on the characteristic curve.
In a Common Emitter transistor circuit if β = 100 and IB = 50µA, compute the values of α, IE and IC.
With the help of neat diagram, explain the operation and characteristics of n-channel enhancement type MOSFET.
Mention the differences between depletion and enhancement MOSFET.
